entities.name: kratu
entities.cite: Kratu — a Vedic word for sacrifice, but here in its older sense of power, resolve, energy — names the word in its unmanifest state

entities.name: aṇḍabhāva
entities.cite: anda-bhavam ivapannah, come as it were to egg-hood

entities.name: vṛtti
entities.cite: Its vrtti, functioning, kriyabhuta, turned into activity

entities.name: kriyā
entities.cite: Its vrtti, functioning, kriyabhuta, turned into activity

entities.name: kramam
entities.cite: labhate kramam bhagasah — takes on sequence limb by limb

entities.name: bhāga
entities.cite: the parts of the word are all 'there' in the sphota as the bird is in the egg

axioms.cite: Kratu — a Vedic word for sacrifice, but here in its older sense of power, resolve, energy — names the word in its unmanifest state

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: anda-bhavam ivapannah, come as it were to egg-hood

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: Its vrtti, functioning, kriyabhuta, turned into activity

axioms.name: kramam
axioms.cite: labhate kramam bhagasah — takes on sequence limb by limb

axioms.name: sarva_atra
axioms.cite: the parts of the word are all 'there' in the sphota as the bird is in the egg — really recoverable, yet not laid out in time until manifestation unfolds them

denials.name: kramam
denials.cite: how a partless whole can nonetheless ground the appearance of ordered parts

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: having arrived as it were at the condition of an egg

rejected_readings.label: sacrificial_ritual
rejected_readings.rendering: The sacrificial ritual, having arrived at the condition of an unfolding process, performs its function through distinct sequential stages.
rejected_readings.why: Treats kratu as literal sacrifice (a real transformation unfolding in ordered time) rather than power/energy (a latent capacity), which commits to parinamate (transformation) and denies the egg model's insight that parts exist all-at-once in the unmanifest whole.

rejected_readings.label: sequential_manifestation
rejected_readings.rendering: The word unfolds its parts sequentially from the start, each part appearing only after the previous one is completed.
rejected_readings.why: Denies the core egg-model insight: that all parts are 'there' in the unmanifest sphota, really recoverable, but not laid out in time until manifestation. This rendering treats the parts as genuinely absent until their moment, making them not pre-contained in the whole.

rejected_readings.label: discrete_parts
rejected_readings.rendering: The word's parts exist as separate, independent entities that come into being one after another during manifestation.
rejected_readings.why: Misses the egg model's core insight: that all parts are 'there' in the sphota as the bird is in the egg, really recoverable yet not laid out in time. This reading treats parts as genuinely absent until manifestation, not pre-contained in the whole.

notes: The egg simile integrates the latent whole (sphota) with ordered manifestation without requiring real transformation or genuine absence of parts. The contested reading (kratu as sacrificial vs. as power) is noted; the contract follows the Vedic sense of power, but both readings cohere with the simile structure.
